zh.lifeSummary: 當John Daniel Sayer於1482年出生在英國英格蘭艾可可爾斯特可爾斯特 卡素時，他的父親Sir John Sayer26歲，母親Elizabeth Hawkins22歲。他於1506年與Elizabeth Hawkins在英國艾可可爾斯特聖 比特也斯 邱奇結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1563年在他的家鄉享年81歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭艾可可爾斯特可爾斯特 卡素。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Daniel Sayer于1482年出生在英国英格兰艾可可爾斯特可爾斯特 卡素时，他的父亲Sir John Sayer26岁，母亲Elizabeth Hawkins22岁。他于1506年与Elizabeth Hawkins在英国艾可可爾斯特聖 比特也斯 邱奇结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于1563年在他的家乡享年81岁，埋葬在英国英格兰艾可可爾斯特可爾斯特 卡素。

nameMeanings.name: Sayer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: common occupational name from Middle English sayer, a phonetic variant of Sawyer , which in Wales and neighboring English counties may have been reinforced by the synonymous Welsh saer ‘carpenter’ and everywhere by a possible borrowing into Middle English of Old French seieor, saieur ‘sawyer’.

English: rare occupational name perhaps denoting an assayer of metals, or a servant who tasted his lord's food before serving, from Middle English saiour ‘assayer, tester’ (a shortened form of Old French assaiour, ultimately from Late Latin exagium, a derivative of exagmināre ‘to weigh’).
English: rare occupational name for a professional reciter or minstrel, from Middle English seier(e) ‘speaker’.

variants.name: Sayre
meanings: English (Middlesex and Surrey): variant of   Sayer  .

variants.name: Sears
meanings: Irish (Kerry): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Saoghair ‘son of Saoghar’, a borrowing in Anglo-Norman Ireland of the Anglo-Norman French and Middle English personal name Saher, Seier (Old French Seheri), from ancient Germanic Sigeheri, Sighari, composed of the elements sig‘victory’ + hari, heri ‘army’. See Sayer 5.
English: variant of   Sayers  .
Americanized form of French (or French Canadian) Cyr and French Saint-Cyr (see St. Cyr ), with the addition of excrescent -s, a common feature of Americanized surnames.

variants.name: Sear
meanings: English: variant of   Sayer  .
Americanized form of French (or French Canadian)   Cyr  .

variants.name: Searson
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Saher, Seier (see Sayer 5) + -son.

English: variant of Searston, a habitational name from Syerston (Nottinghamshire). The placename probably derives from the Old English personal name Sighere (genitive Sigheres) + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.
Irish (Tipperary): variant of   Sears  .

variants.name: Sare
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Sayer  .
English: variant of   Sara  .
Croatian (Šare): nickname derived from šar(en) ‘mottled, gaily colored’, possibly applied to someone with hair of different colors. Compare Share .

variants.name: Saier
meanings: South German: occupational name for a farmer, from an agent derivative of Middle High German saien ‘to sow’. Compare Sayer 8.

variants.name: Say
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Sai in Orne or Say in Indre, France, perhaps so called from a Gaulish personal name Saius + the Latin locative suffix -acum (see also French name 3 below).

English: nickname, perhaps for someone who made or sold say, a type of woollen cloth, or for an habitual wearer of clothes made of this material, from Middle English sai(e) ‘say’ (Old French saie, from Latin saga, plural of sagum ‘military cloak’). See also Sayer .

French (southern): topographic name from saix (from Latin saxum) ‘rock’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example, Say in Loire, Saix in Tarn and Vienne, Le Saix in Hautes-Alpes, or Les Saix in Isère.

variants.name: Sayers
meanings: English: variant of Sayer , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. This name is also established in Ireland.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Saoghair; see Sears .

variants.name: Sawyer
meanings: English: occupational name for someone who earned his living by sawing wood, from Middle English sauer(e), sauw(i)er, also sagh(i)er, sag(i)er ‘sawyer’, a derivative of Old English sagu ‘saw’.

Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname, or translation into English of Jewish Seger or some other surname meaning ‘sawyer’, e.g. German Sager and Slovenian Žagar (see Zagar ).

variants.name: Seekings
meanings: English: from the unrecorded Middle English personal name Saykin, a pet form of Middle English and Anglo-Norman French Saher, Seier (see Sayer 5). The final -s is post-medieval and excrescent.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

nameMeanings.name: Daniel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name (meaning ‘God is my judge’ in Hebrew), borne by the prophet whose story is told in the Book of Daniel. He was an Israelite slave of the Assyrian king Nebuchadnezzar, who obtained great favour through his skill in interpreting dreams and the ‘writing on the wall’ at the feast held by Nebuchadnezzar's son Belshazzar. His enemies managed to get him cast into a lions' den, but he was saved by God. This was a favourite tale in the Middle Ages, often represented in miracle plays. The name has been perennially popular among English speakers since the 16th century and has been particularly favoured since the 1980s.

variants.name: Danyal
meanings: Fanciful respelling of   Daniel .

en.lifeSummary: When John Daniel Sayer was born in 1482, in Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir John Sayer, was 26 and his mother, Elizabeth Hawkins, was 22. He married Elizabeth Hawkins in 1506, in St Peter's Church, Colchester, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died in 1563, in his hometown, at the age of 81, and was buried in Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Daniel Sayer est né en 1482, à Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir John Sayer, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Hawkins, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Hawkins en 1506, à St Peter's Church, Colchester, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé en 1563, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 81 ans, et a été enterré à Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: John Daniel Sayer(이)가 1482년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 코체스터 코체스터 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Sayer(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Hawkins(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1506년 영국 에세스 코체스터 스트 피터'스 c후르치에서 Elizabeth Hawkins(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1563년 그의 고향에서 81세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 코체스터 코체스터 카스틀에 묻혔다.

Ele faleceu em 1563, em sua cidade natal, com 81 anos, e foi sepultado em Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Daniel Sayer родился в 1482 г. в местности колчестир кастл, колчестир, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir John Sayer было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Hawkins, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Hawkins в 1506 г. в местности ст питир'с чурч, колчестир, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер в 1563 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 81 год и был похоронен в местности колчестир кастл, колчестир, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, In: Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Daniel Sayer nació en 1482, en Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir John Sayer, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Hawkins, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Hawkins en 1506, en St Peter's Church, Colchester, Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió en 1563, en su pueblo, a la edad de 81 años, y fue sepultado en Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
